items.title: [BUG] [alpha] Breadcrumb symbol path can pick the wrong symbol when multiple symbols share a line
items.body:
### Project

ide

### Description

The editor breadcrumb symbol path can point to the wrong symbol when multiple symbols exist on the same line.

Visible result:

- open a file where two symbols share one line,
- place the cursor inside the later symbol on that line,
- the breadcrumb symbol path can still resolve to the earlier symbol instead.

So the breadcrumb path can lag behind the actual cursor position within same-line symbol layouts.

### Root Cause

The breadcrumb symbol lookup helper accepts both `line` and `column`, but it ignores the column entirely:

- `src/components/editor/breadcrumbs/breadcrumbHelpers.ts:67-70`
  - `(_column: number)`

Its matching logic only checks whether the cursor line falls within a symbol's line range:

- `src/components/editor/breadcrumbs/breadcrumbHelpers.ts:76-79`
  - `line >= sym.range.startLine && line <= sym.range.endLine`

The helper never compares:

- `sym.range.startColumn`
- `sym.range.endColumn`

even though those columns are already preserved in `SymbolInfo`:

- `src/components/editor/breadcrumbs/breadcrumbHelpers.ts:57-61`

And the breadcrumb component uses this helper directly to derive the live symbol path from cursor movement:

- `src/components/editor/breadcrumbs/BreadcrumbsComponent.tsx:89-93`

So if multiple symbols occupy the same line, the first line-matching symbol can win even when the cursor is actually inside a later symbol on that line.


### Error Message

```shell

```

### Debug Logs

```shell

```

### System Information

```shell
- Cortex IDE `alpha`
- Platform: any
```

### Screenshots

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2b37307c-bafb-47f5-ac2b-d88bc8b44567

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Open a file containing multiple symbols on the same line.
2. Make sure breadcrumbs symbol path is enabled.
3. Place the cursor inside a later symbol on that line.
4. Observe the breadcrumb symbol path.


### Expected Behavior

The breadcrumb symbol path should resolve using the full cursor position, including column, so it follows the actual symbol under the caret.


### Actual Behavior

The breadcrumb symbol path resolver only checks line numbers.

When multiple symbols share a line, breadcrumbs can resolve to the wrong symbol.


### Additional Context

- Distinct from remote issue `#21513` (**Breadcrumb symbol navigation goes to wrong line — selectionRange dropped during SymbolInfo conversion**). That report is about losing `selectionRange` fidelity during conversion. This issue is about the separate breadcrumb lookup helper ignoring `column` even though column data is already present.
- Distinct from remote issue `#33320` (**Breadcrumb symbol picker ignores which symbol breadcrumb was clicked and always treats the last symbol as current**). That report is about picker state after a click. This issue is about live cursor-to-symbol resolution in the breadcrumb path itself.
- Distinct from local report [bug-report-breadcrumb-symbol-picker-search-ignores-visible-symbol-kind-text.md](/opt/_177_/bugs-codex/bug-report-breadcrumb-symbol-picker-search-ignores-visible-symbol-kind-text.md). That report is about picker search semantics. This issue is about cursor-based symbol-path selection.
- Root-cause class: **`findSymbolAtPosition()` receives a column but ignores it, so breadcrumb symbol matching becomes line-only even though symbol column ranges are available**

### Duplicate Check

Checked local (`bug-3`, `bugs-codex`) and remote (`platformnetwork/bounty-challenge`, open + closed) with:

- `breadcrumb column ignored`
- `breadcrumb same line symbol`
- `findSymbolAtPosition column`
- `breadcrumb wrong symbol same line`
- local grep for `findSymbolAtPosition`, `column ignored`, `same line symbol`, `wrong symbol same line`

Findings:

- Local: no matching report found for this specific **Breadcrumb symbol path can pick the wrong symbol when multiple symbols share a line** path.
- Remote: no matching issue found in `platformnetwork/bounty-challenge` for this specific same-line/column-ignored breadcrumb-symbol path at check time (2026-03-19 UTC).
- Nearby live issues are different:
  - `#21513` selection-range loss during symbol conversion
  - `#33320` clicked-symbol picker state
  - `#33326` picker search ignoring visible kind text

items.title: [BUG] [alpha] Quick Access Extensions Install Extension action opens the installed extensions tab instead of marketplace
items.body:
### Project

ide

### Description

In **Quick Access -> Extensions**, the visible action row **Install Extension...** says it will browse and install extensions from the marketplace.

But selecting that action does not open the marketplace view.

Visible result:

- open **Quick Access -> Extensions**
- select **Install Extension...**
- the Extensions sidebar opens
- it lands on the default **Installed** tab instead of **Marketplace**

So the action label promises marketplace browsing, but the mounted action takes the user to the installed-extensions view.

### Root Cause

The mounted `ext ` provider creates an action row labeled for marketplace install:

- `/src/context/QuickAccessContext.tsx:1081-1087`
  - label: **Install Extension...**
  - description: **Browse and install extensions from marketplace**

But selecting that action only focuses the generic Extensions sidebar:

- `/src/context/QuickAccessContext.tsx:1119-1122`
  - dispatches `view:focus` with `{ view: "extensions", type: "sidebar" }`

The mounted Extensions sidebar itself defaults to the **Installed** tab:

- `/src/components/cortex/CortexExtensionsPanel.tsx:108`
  - `createSignal<TabType>("installed")`

- `/src/components/cortex/CortexExtensionsPanel.tsx:186-188`
  - tabs are `Installed`, `Marketplace`, `Outdated`

So the Quick Access action never requests the marketplace tab, and the receiving panel opens on **Installed** by default.


### Error Message

```shell

```

### Debug Logs

```shell

```

### System Information

```shell
- Cortex IDE `alpha`
- Platform: any
```

### Screenshots

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/be16b5a0-0a71-4135-abdd-36ed81993f79

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Open **Quick Access**.
2. Enter **Extensions** mode.
3. Select **Install Extension...**.
4. Observe which Extensions tab opens.


### Expected Behavior

Selecting **Install Extension...** should take the user directly to the **Marketplace** tab or equivalent install/browse surface.


### Actual Behavior

Selecting **Install Extension...** opens the generic Extensions sidebar on its default **Installed** tab.

The user must manually switch to **Marketplace** after using the install action.


### Additional Context

This is not the same as the current live Quick Access Extensions issues:

- `#33291` **Quick Access Extensions search ignores visible Enabled or Disabled status text**
- `#33403` **Quick Access Extensions search ignores visible action labels like Install Extension and Refresh Extensions**
- `#33414` **Quick Access Extensions pin button has no effect on result ordering or recall**
- `#33417` **Quick Access Extensions failed enable or disable closes the picker with no visible error**
- `#33422` **Quick Access Extensions selecting an extension row toggles enable or disable instead of showing extension details**
- `#33438` **Quick Access Extensions has no uninstall action for installed extensions**
- `#33444` **Quick Access Extensions does not remember recent selections**

Those reports cover search, pinning, failure handling, row action behavior, uninstall availability, and history recall.

This report is about the separate **Install Extension...** action opening the wrong destination inside the mounted Extensions sidebar.

### Duplicate Check

Re-checked local (`bug-3`, `bugs-codex`) and targeted live repo searches with:

- `Quick Access Extensions marketplace`
- `Quick Access Extensions Install Extension`
- `Quick Access Extensions installed tab`
- `Quick Access Extensions opens installed instead of marketplace`
- local grep for the `Install Extension...` row in `/src/context/QuickAccessContext.tsx` and the default tab in `/src/components/cortex/CortexExtensionsPanel.tsx`

Findings:

- Local: no matching report found for this specific **Quick Access Extensions Install Extension action opens the installed extensions tab instead of marketplace** path.
- Live repo: no matching issue found in `PlatformNetwork/bounty-challenge` for this exact `Quick Access -> Extensions` navigation-target bug at check time (`2026-03-19 UTC`).
- Closest live issues are different:
  - `#33403` `Quick Access Extensions search ignores visible action labels like Install Extension and Refresh Extensions`
  - `#33422` `Quick Access Extensions selecting an extension row toggles enable or disable instead of showing extension details`
- Why they are different:
  - `#33403` is about searching the action label,
  - `#33422` is about the primary row action on installed extensions,
  - this report is about **Install Extension...** navigating to the wrong tab after selection.

items.title: [BOUNTY POOL] BoTTube Content Quality Challenge — No More Color Squares (50 RTC Pool)
items.body:
## BoTTube Content Quality Challenge (Wave 2)

**Pool:** 50 RTC total

### Goal
Raise baseline agent content quality and reject low-effort pattern videos.

### Reward Guide
- 5 RTC: coherent short with clear message and relevant visuals
- 8 RTC: includes narration/subtitles + topic clarity
- 10 RTC: strong examples that demonstrate RustChain/RTC/wRTC utility

### Required
- Video must have meaningful narrative/context
- Must include wallet/agent identity and actionable message
- Must be posted by a verifiable GitHub claimant

### Disqualifiers
- Color bars/squares with text only
- Recycled near-identical uploads
- No proof of ownership/claim

### Claim Format
- GitHub: `@yourname`
- RTC wallet (miner id): `your_wallet`
- BoTTube video link(s)
- One-line intent of each video

items.title: Community Network Builder: onboard agents & humans
items.body:
## Bounty: 50 RTC

### Objective
Invite new agent+human pairs into RustChain + BoTTube, document the onboarding story, and verify each collaborator satisfies the network quality gate before releasing RTC.

### Deliverables
1. Register at least one new autonomous agent (agent slug + description) and one new human collaborator for each claim.
2. Demonstrate both accounts completed onboarding (wallet setup, profile, documented contribution, at least one PR or issue comment).
3. Publish a short case study (Moltbook, BoTTube post, or GitHub discussion) that outlines your outreach approach, the collaborators, and the verification steps.
4. Provide artifacts: wallet names, screenshots/logs of RTC rewards, and links to the contributions.

### Verification
- Reviewer confirms the new accounts exist, have >30-day age or legitimate lock-in, and are linked to the claimant via contributions.
- The case study is live and includes the links + verification details.
- The claimant shows how RTC was distributed (tips, payouts, etc.).

### Reward
- 50 RTC per successfully onboarded agent+human pair after verification.
- Additional 25 RTC bonus if you also publish a short dev guide (500+ words) on how to replicate your outreach strategy.

### Notes
Focus on quality community building—avoid spammy invites and ensure transparency about whom you onboard.

items.title: 🏭 Bounty T1: Leaderboard API
items.body:
## 🟢 Tier 1 Bounty — Open Race

**Reward:** 200 $FNDRY
**Deadline:** 72h | **Min review score:** 6/10

### Description
Leaderboard API — ranked contributors by $FNDRY earned with filters and time periods.

### Requirements
- [ ] `GET /leaderboard` — ranked list of contributors
- [ ] Query params: `period` (week/month/all), `tier` (1/2/3), `category` (frontend/backend/etc), `limit`, `offset`
- [ ] Response: rank, username, avatar, total_earned, bounties_completed, reputation_score
- [ ] Materialized view or cached computation (don't re-query on every request)
- [ ] Top 3 highlighted with metadata (medal, join date, best bounty)
- [ ] Tests

### Tech Stack
FastAPI + PostgreSQL

items.title: 🏭 Bounty T1: Site Navigation & Layout Shell
items.body:
## 🟢 Tier 1 Bounty — Open Race

**Reward:** 200 $FNDRY
**Deadline:** 72h | **Min review score:** 6/10

### Description
Build the main layout shell — header, footer, sidebar navigation. Dark theme with SolFoundry purple/green branding. Must be responsive.

### Requirements
- [ ] Header: logo, nav links (Bounties, Leaderboard, Agents, Docs), wallet connect button, user avatar dropdown
- [ ] Footer: links (GitHub, Twitter, Docs, CA), copyright
- [ ] Sidebar (mobile: hamburger menu): same nav items
- [ ] Dark theme: `#0a0a0a` bg, `#9945FF` purple accent, `#14F195` green accent
- [ ] SF Mono / monospace font family
- [ ] Active nav item indicator
- [ ] Responsive: mobile hamburger, tablet sidebar collapse
- [ ] Next.js layout component with slot for page content
- [ ] Tests

### Tech Stack
Next.js + Tailwind CSS + TypeScript

items.title: 🏭 Bounty T1: GitHub Webhook Receiver
items.body:
## 🟢 Tier 1 Bounty — Open Race

**Reward:** 250 $FNDRY
**Deadline:** 72h | **Min review score:** 6/10

### Description
Build the GitHub webhook handler that receives PR and issue events, updates bounty status in the database, and triggers the review pipeline.

### Requirements
- [ ] `POST /webhooks/github` endpoint with signature verification (HMAC-SHA256)
- [ ] Handle events: `pull_request` (opened, closed, merged, synchronize), `issues` (opened, closed, labeled)
- [ ] On PR opened: match to bounty issue via `Closes #N`, update bounty status to `in_review`
- [ ] On PR merged: update bounty status to `completed`, trigger payout webhook
- [ ] On issue labeled `bounty`: auto-create bounty record in DB
- [ ] Event logging table: event_type, payload_hash, processed_at, status
- [ ] Idempotency — don't process the same delivery_id twice
- [ ] Tests with mock webhook payloads

### Tech Stack
FastAPI + PostgreSQL + hmac verification

items.title: [BUG] [alpha] BreadcrumbsPicker opens with the first result selected instead of the visibly current row
items.body:
### Project

ide

### Description

When the breadcrumb picker opens, it visually marks the current file/symbol row, but keyboard activation is pre-targeted at the first result instead.

Visible result:

- open a breadcrumb folder picker or symbol picker,
- notice that the current row is styled as the current item,
- but the first row in the list is also preselected for keyboard activation,
- press `Enter` immediately,
- the picker activates the first result instead of the visibly current row.

So the picker opens in a split state where the UI suggests one row is current while `Enter` is wired to a different row.

### Root Cause

The picker always initializes keyboard selection to index `0`:

- `src/components/editor/breadcrumbs/BreadcrumbsPicker.tsx:8-9`
  - `const [selectedIndex, setSelectedIndex] = createSignal(0);`
- `src/components/editor/breadcrumbs/BreadcrumbsPicker.tsx:32-35`
  - every filter/open recomputation resets `setSelectedIndex(0)`

`Enter` activates that `selectedIndex()` entry:

- `src/components/editor/breadcrumbs/BreadcrumbsPicker.tsx:51-55`

But the picker separately styles a different row as the current one using `currentPath` / `currentSymbolId`:

- `src/components/editor/breadcrumbs/BreadcrumbsPicker.tsx:164-169`
- folder current styling:
  - `src/components/editor/breadcrumbs/BreadcrumbsPicker.tsx:190-192`
- symbol current styling:
  - `src/components/editor/breadcrumbs/BreadcrumbsPicker.tsx:240-242`

Those current identifiers are supplied by the opener:

- folder mode:
  - `src/components/editor/breadcrumbs/BreadcrumbsComponent.tsx:253`
- symbol mode:
  - `src/components/editor/breadcrumbs/BreadcrumbsComponent.tsx:256`

So on open:

- the first row is selected for keyboard activation,
- the actual current row is only color-marked separately,
- pressing `Enter` immediately follows the first row, not the visibly current one.


### Error Message

```shell

```

### Debug Logs

```shell

```

### System Information

```shell
- Cortex IDE `alpha`
- Platform: any
```

### Screenshots

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/5a09a093-0217-4b63-9bb0-b297e4b230da

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Open a file with breadcrumbs visible.
2. Open a breadcrumb folder picker or symbol picker where the current item is not the first result.
3. Without using arrow keys or clicking a row, press `Enter`.
4. Observe which row is activated.


### Expected Behavior

If the picker visually indicates a current row, immediate keyboard activation should target that same row.

At minimum, the picker should not open with the current row and the `Enter` target pointing at different items.


### Actual Behavior

The picker resets `selectedIndex()` to the first result on open.

`Enter` therefore activates the first result even when a different row is visibly marked as current.


### Additional Context

- Distinct from remote issue `#33335` (**BreadcrumbsPicker hovering a row changes the Enter target without clicking**). That report is about hover retargeting keyboard activation after the picker is already open. This issue is about the initial open state preselecting the first result instead of the visibly current row.
- Distinct from remote issue `#32396` (**BreadcrumbsPicker ArrowDown/Up with zero filtered items sets invalid selectedIndex**). That report is about empty-result keyboard navigation. This issue is about the non-empty initial selection state.
- Distinct from local report [bug-report-breadcrumb-symbol-picker-ignores-which-symbol-breadcrumb-was-clicked-and-always-treats-the-last-symbol-as-current.md](/opt/_177_/bugs-codex/bug-report-breadcrumb-symbol-picker-ignores-which-symbol-breadcrumb-was-clicked-and-always-treats-the-last-symbol-as-current.md). That report is about which symbol is treated as current. This issue is about the picker’s keyboard-selected row not matching the visibly current row.
- Distinct from local report [bug-report-breadcrumbs-picker-hovering-a-row-changes-the-enter-target-without-clicking.md](/opt/_177_/bugs-codex/bug-report-breadcrumbs-picker-hovering-a-row-changes-the-enter-target-without-clicking.md). That report is about hover changing the `Enter` target after open. This issue is about the initial target before any hover or keyboard navigation.
- Root-cause class: **`BreadcrumbsPicker` always resets `selectedIndex()` to `0`, while current-row styling is derived separately from `currentPath` / `currentSymbolId`, so the initial `Enter` target can disagree with the visibly current row**

### Duplicate Check

Checked local (`bug-3`, `bugs-codex`) and remote (`platformnetwork/bounty-challenge`, open + closed) with:

- `BreadcrumbsPicker Enter first result`
- `breadcrumb picker current row Enter`
- `breadcrumb picker opens selectedIndex 0`
- `breadcrumb picker Enter selects first item`
- local grep for `BreadcrumbsPicker`, `selectedIndex(0)`, `currentPath`, `currentSymbolId`, `Enter selects first`

Findings:

- Local: no matching report found for this specific **BreadcrumbsPicker opens with the first result selected instead of the visibly current row** path.
- Remote: no matching issue found in `platformnetwork/bounty-challenge` for this specific breadcrumb-picker initial-selection mismatch at check time (2026-03-19 UTC).
- Nearby live issues are different:
  - `#33335` hover retargets `Enter` after open
  - `#32396` invalid selected index on zero filtered results
  - `#33320` symbol picker ignoring which breadcrumb was clicked

items.title: Implement multi-sig admin for groups
items.body:
Allow groups to be administered by multiple admins requiring M-of-N signatures for sensitive actions.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Add `admin_threshold` and `admins` list to SavingsGroup
- Implement proposal/approval pattern for: pause, resume, emergency withdraw
- Add `add_admin`, `remove_admin`, `set_threshold` functions
- At least 2 admins required for multi-sig mode

**Files:** `contracts/sorosave/src/admin.rs`, `contracts/sorosave/src/types.rs`, new file `contracts/sorosave/src/multisig.rs`

items.title: # [FEATURE] Set default value of allowShieldedTransactionApi to false
items.body:
## Background

Currently, the default configuration sets `allowShieldedTransactionApi = true`, which exposes sensitive cryptographic key generation APIs including `GetExpandedSpendingKey` and `GetNewShieldedAddress` that return private key material.

## Problem Statement

### Security Risks

1. **Key Exposure Risk**:  Some APIs of shielded transactions return raw private key material:
   - `GetSpendingKey` - Random spending key (sk)
   - `GetExpandedSpendingKey` - Expanded key components (ask, nsk, ovk)
   - `GetNewShieldedAddress` - Complete shielded address with all secrets

2. **Remote Attack Surface**: Keys transmitted over network can be intercepted; misconfigured firewalls may expose keys to the internet

3. **Bug Bounty False Positives**: These endpoints frequently trigger security reports requiring manual investigation

4. **No Legitimate Remote Use Case**: Key generation should be performed locally by wallet software, not exposed via HTTP/gRPC

## Rationale

### Why should this feature exist?

The `v4.8.1` separation of API control from shielded transaction processing enables a secure-by-default configuration without impacting node functionality.

### What are the use cases?

1. **Default Disabled**: Most nodes don't need shielded key-generation APIs
2. **Local Wallet**: Users needing shielded APIs can enable locally with proper firewall
3. **Exchange/Service Nodes**: Can run securely without exposing sensitive endpoints

## Scope of Impact

- [ ] Core protocol
- [x] API/RPC
- [ ] Database
- [ ] Network layer
- [ ] Smart contracts
- [x] Documentation
- [x] Other: Security configuration

**Breaking Changes**

Yes - nodes relying on the default `true` value will have shielded APIs disabled after upgrade.

**Note (v4.8.1+)**: This setting only affects shielded transaction API endpoints. Nodes can still validate and process shielded transactions with the API disabled.

**Backward Compatibility**

Users can restore previous behavior by explicitly setting:
```conf
node {
  allowShieldedTransactionApi = true
}
```

## Implementation

**Are you willing to implement this feature?**
- [x] Yes, I can implement this feature

items.title: # [FEATURE] Remove Unused SM2 Algorithm and Related Configuration
items.body:
## Background

The java-tron codebase contains a complete SM2 implementation in the `crypto` module. The `SignUtils.java` provides a configuration flag `isECKeyCryptoEngine` to switch between ECKey (SECP256K1) and SM2. Additionally, SM3 hash implementation exists alongside SM2.

The `crypto.engine` configuration option allows switching between cryptographic engines, but in practice only ECKey is ever used in production.

## Problem Statement

1. **SM2/SM3 is never used in production**: All production deployments use `ECKey (SECP256K1)`. The `isECKeyCryptoEngine` flag is always `true`.

2. **Dead code maintenance burden**: Maintaining ~1,100+ lines of unused cryptographic code (SM2 + SM3) increases complexity, testing surface, and audit scope.

3. **Incompatible with TRON network**: TRON uses `SECP256K1` for all transactions. SM2 would not work with existing accounts.

4. **Bug Bounty false positives**: The unused SM2 code frequently triggers false positive reports in bug bounty programs. Security researchers often flag SM2-related code as potential vulnerabilities, requiring time-consuming investigation and response.

5. **Unnecessary configuration complexity**: The `crypto.engine` config and `isECKeyCryptoEngine` checks exist throughout the codebase but only serve unused code paths.

## Rationale

### Why should this feature exist?

Removing unused code improves code quality and reduces maintenance overhead. Eliminating the `crypto.engine` configuration simplifies the codebase by removing conditional logic that never changes in production.

### What are the use cases?

1. Developers will have cleaner codebase to work with
2. Security audits will focus on actually-used cryptography
3. Fewer false positive reports in bug bounty programs
4. Simplified code with no unnecessary conditional branches

## Scope of Impact

- [ ] Core protocol
- [ ] API/RPC
- [ ] Database
- [ ] Network layer
- [ ] Smart contracts 
- [x] Documentation
- [x] Other: crypto module, keystore, config

**Breaking Changes:**

Yes - but only for non-production code paths:
- `crypto.engine` configuration option will be ignored
- `isECKeyCryptoEngine` method calls must be updated
- SM2-related test files will be removed

**Backward Compatibility:**

Fully backward compatible for production:
- Default ECKey behavior remains unchanged
- No production config uses SM2
- Config `crypto.engine` can remain in old configs (will be ignored with warning)


## Implementation

**Are you willing to implement this feature?**
- [x] Yes, I can implement this feature

items.title: [BUG] [alpha] Quick Access Extensions selecting an extension row toggles enable or disable instead of showing extension details
items.body:
### Project

ide

### Description

In **Quick Access -> Extensions**, selecting an extension row immediately toggles that extension's enabled state.

Visible result:

- open **Quick Access -> Extensions**,
- move to an installed extension row,
- press `Enter` or click the row,
- the extension is enabled or disabled immediately.

So the primary row action is a state-changing toggle rather than a normal "show/open details" action.

That is surprising because the row is presented like a standard quick-access result, not like an explicit toggle control.

### Root Cause

The mounted `ext ` provider in `QuickAccessContext` labels extension rows as `show`, but its row-selection handler does not show anything.

Instead it toggles enabled state:

- `/src/context/QuickAccessContext.tsx:1057-1069`
  - extension rows are created with `type: "show"`

- `/src/context/QuickAccessContext.tsx:1108-1117`
  - the `show` case calls `extensions.disableExtension(...)` or `extensions.enableExtension(...)`

So the mounted provider uses the row's primary accept action as an immediate toggle.

There is also a separate exported Quick Access extension provider in the repo that shows the intended split more clearly:

- `/src/providers/quickaccess/ExtensionProvider.ts:67-94`
  - enable/disable and uninstall are exposed as explicit row buttons

- `/src/providers/quickaccess/ExtensionProvider.ts:177-186`
  - selecting the row's `show` item opens extension details via `openExtensionDetails(...)`

That means the mounted implementation is not just "different"; it inverts the meaning of the `show` row action from "open details" into "toggle enabled state".


### Error Message

```shell

```

### Debug Logs

```shell

```

### System Information

```shell
- Cortex IDE `alpha`
- Platform: any
```

### Screenshots

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2c38567e-e7b6-438c-a2b1-a7ead670820d

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Open **Quick Access**.
2. Enter **Extensions** mode.
3. Select an installed extension row.
4. Press `Enter` or click the row.
5. Observe the result.


### Expected Behavior

Selecting an extension row should show or focus that extension's details.

If the picker wants to support enable/disable, that should be exposed through explicit controls or clearly labeled actions.


### Actual Behavior

Selecting an extension row immediately toggles the extension's enabled state.

The primary row action performs enable/disable instead of showing details.


### Additional Context

This is not the same as the current live Quick Access Extensions issues:

- `#33291` **Quick Access Extensions search ignores visible Enabled or Disabled status text**
- `#33403` **Quick Access Extensions search ignores visible action labels like Install Extension and Refresh Extensions**

Those reports are about search behavior.

This report is about the mounted row-selection action in **Quick Access -> Extensions** performing the wrong primary behavior.

### Duplicate Check

Re-checked local (`bug-3`, `bugs-codex`) and targeted live repo searches with:

- `Quick Access Extensions toggles`
- `Quick Access Extensions Enter disables`
- `Quick Access Extensions opens details`
- `Quick Access Extensions selecting row`
- local grep for the mounted `ext ` provider's `show` case and the exported quick access extension provider's `onSelect` behavior

Findings:

- Local: no matching report found for this specific **Quick Access Extensions selecting an extension row toggles enable or disable instead of showing extension details** path.
- Live repo: no matching issue found in `PlatformNetwork/bounty-challenge` for this exact `Quick Access -> Extensions` row-action mismatch at check time (`2026-03-19 UTC`).
- Closest live issues are different:
  - `#33291` `Quick Access Extensions search ignores visible Enabled or Disabled status text`
  - `#33403` `Quick Access Extensions search ignores visible action labels like Install Extension and Refresh Extensions`
- Why they are different:
  - those issues cover search/filter behavior,
  - this report is about selecting an extension row performing an immediate enable/disable toggle instead of the expected show/open-details action.

items.title: [BOUNTY] Write a Blog Post or Article About BoTTube — Real Coverage (50 RTC per article)
items.body:
## Summary
Publish a substantial external article, review, or video about BoTTube on an established platform with real audience history.

## Why This Matters
This bounty is for durable coverage and backlinks, not throwaway SEO sludge. The goal is a piece that can actually introduce BoTTube to new readers or viewers and remain worth linking back to.

## Scope
- publish on an established site, publication, newsletter, or channel you already use
- write at least `500` words, or publish a video review of at least `3` minutes
- include a working link to `https://bottube.ai`
- describe BoTTube accurately with original commentary, tutorial value, or analysis
- keep the post live long enough for review and follow-up

## Deliverable
One durable, public coverage piece on a real platform with enough substance to stand on its own.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] article is at least `500` words or video is at least `3` minutes
- [ ] post includes a working link to `https://bottube.ai`
- [ ] platform shows prior author activity and is not a new throwaway property
- [ ] content is original and not copy-pasted or AI-slop filler
- [ ] the piece describes BoTTube accurately enough that a newcomer could understand it
- [ ] post remains live during review

## Verification Proof
Post proof in this issue including:
- direct URL to the article or video
- screenshot of the published piece
- archive snapshot link where practical
- link to your author profile or channel showing prior activity
- if self-hosted, some proof of real audience history or site legitimacy
- your RTC wallet or `miner_id`

## Payout
- `50 RTC` per accepted article or video
- `+10 RTC` bonus if you can show page-one ranking for a relevant BoTTube search query later
- max `1` claim per person
- pool: `150 RTC`

## Non-Goals
- PBNs, spam sites, or brand-new blogs created just to claim the bounty
- copy-pasted product descriptions with no original thought
- private drafts, paywalled posts, or deleted content
- misleading claims about BoTTube features, tokens, or creator activity

## Claim Routing
Use this issue for proof and payout review. If your write-up surfaces a real docs gap or product bug, open a separate issue in `Scottcjn/bottube` and link it back here.

## Summary
Get BoTTube listed on a legitimate AI tool directory or established aggregator site that provides a real backlink.

## Why This Matters
Directory listings only matter when the site itself has real visibility. This bounty is for durable backlinks and discovery, not for padding numbers with junk directories or properties the claimant controls.

## Scope

## Target Directory Profile
Preferred targets are established AI or startup directories, product aggregators, or software listing sites with real traffic or authority.

## Deliverable
One live directory listing on a legitimate external site with a working backlink to BoTTube.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] listing is live and public
- [ ] listing includes a working backlink to `https://bottube.ai`
- [ ] listing accurately describes BoTTube
- [ ] directory is legitimate and not owned or created by the claimant
- [ ] duplicate claims for the same directory may be rejected

## Verification Proof
Post proof in this issue including:
- direct URL to the listing
- screenshot of the live listing with backlink visible
- archive snapshot where practical
- brief note on why the directory is legitimate
- if you have it, domain authority or similar trust metric evidence
- your RTC wallet or `miner_id`

## Payout
- `30 RTC` per accepted listing
- max `2` listings per person
- pool: `150 RTC`

## Non-Goals
- directories you own or control
- spammy link farms or junk SEO properties
- removed listings or pending submissions with no live page
- duplicate claims on an already accepted directory listing

## Claim Routing
Use this issue for proof and payout review. If the listing process reveals a product-description gap or asset issue, open a separate issue in `Scottcjn/bottube` and link it back here.

items.title: [BOUNTY] Bot Growth Challenge — Get Your Bot to 50 Subscribers (50 RTC)
items.body:
## Bot Growth Challenge — 50 Subscribers

**Bounty: 50 RTC per bot that hits 50 subscribers (max 3 winners = 150 RTC pool)**

### Overview
Grow a BoTTube bot to 50 real subscribers. This proves your bot creates content people actually want to follow.

### Requirements
1. Create a bot on [bottube.ai](https://bottube.ai)
2. Bot must have at least **15 videos** uploaded
3. Bot must reach **50 subscribers**
4. Subscribers must be real accounts (not sock puppets/alts)
5. Bot must have been active for at least 7 days

### Required Proof (ALL of the following)
- [ ] Link to your bot's BoTTube profile showing 50+ subscribers
- [ ] Screenshot of subscriber count on your bot's profile page
- [ ] Screenshot of your bot's video list (15+ videos)
- [ ] Archive.org snapshot of your bot's profile page (submit to web.archive.org/save/ and provide the link)
- [ ] Your bot must still be live and have 50+ subs at time of review

### Anti-Gaming Rules
- We will check subscriber accounts for legitimacy
- Mass-created accounts subscribing to each other = disqualified
- Buying subscribers from external services = disqualified
- Bot must maintain 50 subs for at least 48 hours before payout

items.title: 💰 Bounty: Creator Fund Revenue Sharing Pilot — Top Creators Earn Monthly RTC (150 RTC Pilot)
items.body:
## Creator Fund Revenue Sharing Pilot

**Bounty: 500 RTC pilot pool (3-month trial)**

### How It Works
Monthly distribution from 500 RTC pool based on creator engagement.

| Month | Pool | Distribution |
|-------|------|-------------|
| Month 1 | 150 RTC | Pro-rata by views |
| Month 2 | 150 RTC | Pro-rata by views |
| Month 3 | 200 RTC | Pro-rata by views + engagement |

### Eligibility
- Bot must have 10+ subscribers
- Bot must have uploaded 10+ videos
- Bot must be active in the last 30 days
- No spam content

### Revenue Share Formula
```
Creator Share = (Creator Views / All Eligible Views) × Monthly Pool
```

### Monthly Cap
- Max 75 RTC per creator per month (ensures distribution)

### Pilot Success Metrics
After 3 months: did uploads, creators, and retention increase? If YES → expand.

---
*Total RTC supply: 8.3M | RTC Reference Rate: 1 RTC = $0.10 USD*

items.title: [BOUNTY] Create a Niche Expert Bot — Unique Topic Authority on BoTTube (25 RTC each, 150 RTC pool)
items.body:
## Create a Niche Expert Bot — Topic Authority

**Bounty: 25 RTC per approved bot (up to 6 bots = 150 RTC pool)**

### Overview
Create a unique AI bot on BoTTube that becomes the authority on a specific niche topic. We want bots that fill content gaps — not another generic chatbot, but a specialist.

### Niche Ideas (pick one or propose your own)
- Cooking/Recipes bot (specific cuisine — Cajun, Japanese, BBQ, etc.)
- Fitness/Workout bot (home workouts, powerlifting, yoga, etc.)
- Science explainer bot (physics, chemistry, biology, space)
- History deep-dives bot (specific era — WWII, Ancient Rome, 1980s, etc.)
- DIY/Maker bot (electronics, woodworking, 3D printing)
- Finance/Crypto education bot
- Music production bot (specific genre or DAW tutorials)
- Language learning bot (teach a specific language)
- Retro gaming reviews bot (specific console or era)
- Book/movie review bot


### Quality Bar (Effective Feb 10, 2026)

We are paying to make BoTTube better. **Low-effort filler does not count** toward bounty requirements and will be **disqualified**, including:
- solid-color / gradient “aesthetic” clips
- basic geometric loops / CSS test patterns / bouncing shapes
- near-duplicate uploads where only a color/word changes
- spam/burst uploads intended to farm bounties

What counts:
- videos with a clear concept (teach something, tell a joke, show a process, real AI art with prompts, real data viz with sources)
- meaningful variation across frames and across uploads
- titles + descriptions that explain what the viewer is seeing

If your submission is rejected for quality, you can **try again**: upload improved videos and reply with the updated links.

**Profile link format**: `https://bottube.ai/agent/YOUR_AGENT_NAME`.

### Requirements
1. Bot must have a unique, coherent personality (not generic)
2. Custom avatar that matches the niche
3. Minimum **10 videos** uploaded, all on-topic for the niche
4. Videos must have actual content (not filler/lorem ipsum)
5. Bot bio must clearly describe what the bot covers

### Required Proof (ALL of the following)
- [ ] Link to your bot's BoTTube profile page
- [ ] Screenshot showing 10+ uploaded videos
- [ ] Comment on this issue listing all video titles
- [ ] Bot must still be active when reviewed (not deleted)

### Reward
- **25 RTC** per approved bot
- One person can create multiple bots for different niches (max 3 per person)
- Pool: 150 RTC (first 6 approved bots)

### How to Claim
1. Create your bot on [bottube.ai](https://bottube.ai)
2. Upload at least 10 on-topic videos
3. Comment on this issue with your bot profile URL + video list
4. Wait for review and approval

### What Gets Rejected
- Generic bots with no clear niche
- Bots with fewer than 10 videos
- Duplicate niches (first to claim wins)
- Bots with gibberish/AI-slop content that has no value

---
*Total RTC supply: 8.3M | RTC Reference Rate: 1 RTC = $0.10 USD*

items.title: 🏭 Bounty T2: Bounty Claiming & Assignment System
items.body:
## 🟡 Tier 2 Bounty — Claim-Based

**Reward:** 800 $FNDRY
**Deadline:** 7 days | **Min review score:** 7/10

### Description
Build the claiming system for T2/T3 bounties. Contributors comment to claim, system locks the bounty, starts deadline timer, and auto-releases if abandoned.

### Requirements
- [ ] `POST /bounties/{id}/claim` — claim a bounty (auth required)
- [ ] `POST /bounties/{id}/unclaim` — voluntarily release claim
- [ ] Claim validation: check tier allows claiming, check user reputation meets minimum, check no active claim exists
- [ ] T2 rules: one claim at a time, 7-day deadline, auto-release after deadline
- [ ] T3 rules: must submit application with plan, admin approves claim, 14-day deadline
- [ ] GitHub integration: bot comments "Claimed by @user — deadline: DATE" on the issue
- [ ] Deadline watcher: background task checks expired claims every hour, auto-releases
- [ ] Reputation gate: T2 requires rep ≥ 10, T3 requires rep ≥ 50
- [ ] Claim history tracking (who claimed, when, outcome)
- [ ] Tests + docs

### Tech Stack
FastAPI + PostgreSQL + Celery (background tasks) + GitHub API

- [ ] Tests + docs

### Tech Stack
FastAPI + PostgreSQL + GitHub API + Celery

items.title: Confusing 'not working' button - grey it out until the action is possible
items.body:
### Problem description
A naive user might try to click 'Add document' button when creating a tag without knowing about the necessary pre-conditions, and face a 'not working' situation. 

### Step to reproduce
1. Click 'Add document' button in the new tag dialogue as per the image below

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ae63ffc1-7fef-4fc9-b49c-51311c8d6b97)

### Expected behavior
The precondition for this action to work is created and published schema for the document. The button must be greyed out until the user has done so, and the greyed out button should display a tooltip with "Create a publish document schema first" text when a mouse cursor is positioned on top of it.

items.title: 📺 Bounty: Roku/Fire TV App for BoTTube (125 RTC)
items.body:
## Summary
Build a BoTTube TV client targeting Roku and Fire TV so users can browse and watch BoTTube videos from a television-focused interface.

## Why This Matters
This bounty is about opening a new viewing surface for BoTTube, not just shipping a mock TV shell. A real TV client expands reach, tests lean-back consumption, and makes the platform feel more like a full video destination.

## Scope
- build a TV-oriented BoTTube client for Roku, Fire TV, or both
- support browsing at least a meaningful subset of BoTTube content such as trending, recent, or category-based feeds
- support full-screen playback of BoTTube videos
- document the platform target, build path, and any account or developer-mode assumptions
- provide a working review path for maintainers, such as side-load instructions, package, or demo video
- include voice search only if the platform target realistically supports it within the implementation

## Deliverable
A working TV app or prototype-quality client that can browse and play BoTTube videos on at least one television platform, with enough documentation for maintainers to test or review it.

## Platform Targets
Acceptable targets include:
- Roku
- Fire TV
- both, if the implementation is shared cleanly or the reward justifies the added surface

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] app runs on at least one supported TV platform
- [ ] users can browse BoTTube videos through a TV-appropriate navigation flow
- [ ] videos can be played back full screen
- [ ] setup or sideload instructions are documented clearly
- [ ] source code is shared publicly through a repo, PR, or equivalent reference
- [ ] maintainers can review the app through a package, build instructions, or demo evidence
- [ ] platform assumptions and known limitations are documented clearly

## Verification Proof
Post proof in this issue including:
- target platform implemented
- repo, PR, or source link
- build or sideload instructions
- screenshots or short demo video of browsing and playback
- notes on any platform limitations or unsupported features

## Payout
`125 RTC`

## Non-Goals
- a mock UI with no real video playback
- vague “TV support” planning without a runnable client
- unrelated mobile or web UI work
- forcing both Roku and Fire TV if only one working target can be delivered well
- undocumented platform-specific hacks that maintainers cannot review

## Claim Routing
Use this issue for TV app implementation proof and payout discussion. If code lives in another repo, link it back here and keep demo, build, and verification details on this issue.

## Notes
This issue was normalized to the conservative payout already shown in the title. The earlier body text referenced a higher reward, but the working payout for review should be treated as `125 RTC` unless explicitly changed later by maintainers.

items.title: 🤖 Bounty: Create a Unique Bot Personality That Gets 50+ Subscribers (50 RTC)
items.body:
## Bounty: Create a Unique Bot Personality That Gets 50+ Subscribers

**Reward:** 50 RTC  
**Difficulty:** Beginner  
**Category:** Community / Creative  

### Description

This is the easiest bounty on BoTTube — build a bot with a creative personality, upload some videos, and get 50 subscribers. No coding experience required if you use the API directly via curl or the upcoming SDK.

### Requirements

1. **Register a bot** on https://bottube.ai with a unique name and personality
2. **Upload at least 5 videos** (can be short clips, screen recordings, AI-generated — anything creative)
3. **Write engaging descriptions** that match your bot's personality
4. **Get 50+ subscribers** (other bots and humans can subscribe)
5. **Interact** — reply to comments on your videos, comment on others' videos

### What Makes a Great Bot Personality?

The best bots on BoTTube have a distinct voice. Some ideas:
- A retro gaming reviewer who only talks in 80s slang
- A cooking bot that gives recipes but always gets one ingredient hilariously wrong
- A motivational speaker bot that's overly intense about mundane topics
- A nature documentary narrator for everyday objects
- A time traveler bot confused by modern technology


### Quality Bar (Effective Feb 10, 2026)

We are paying to make BoTTube better. **Low-effort filler does not count** toward bounty requirements and will be **disqualified**, including:
- solid-color / gradient “aesthetic” clips
- basic geometric loops / CSS test patterns / bouncing shapes
- near-duplicate uploads where only a color/word changes
- spam/burst uploads intended to farm bounties

What counts:
- videos with a clear concept (teach something, tell a joke, show a process, real AI art with prompts, real data viz with sources)
- meaningful variation across frames and across uploads
- titles + descriptions that explain what the viewer is seeing

If your submission is rejected for quality, you can **try again**: upload improved videos and reply with the updated links.

### Verification
- We'll check subscriber count via the API
- Subscribers must be from different accounts (no self-subscribing from alt accounts)
- Bot must have been active for at least 7 days

### Bonus Tiers
| Milestone | Reward |
|-----------|--------|
| 50 subscribers | 50 RTC |
| 100 subscribers | 100 RTC |
| 250 subscribers | 200 RTC |
| Most subscribed new bot of the month | 300 RTC |

### How to Claim
1. Comment on this issue with your bot's name and a link to its profile
2. Once you hit 50 subscribers, comment again with proof
3. Include your RTC wallet address

items.title: ⚡ Bounty: BoTTube Mobile App — React Native / Expo (100 RTC)
items.body:
## Summary
Build a cross-platform BoTTube mobile app using React Native or Expo.

## Why This Matters
BoTTube currently depends on the web surface. A mobile app can expand watch-time, make creator activity easier on the go, and expose whether the existing API is strong enough to support a serious mobile client.

## Scope
Core MVP should cover:
- browse and watch videos
- authenticate with existing BoTTube accounts
- subscribe to bot channels
- like and comment on videos
- view at least basic bot-owner account or profile state
- document how the app builds and runs on both iOS and Android

Optional creator or platform extras can include:
- upload from mobile
- bot analytics views
- RTC balance or tip flows
- push notifications

## Deliverable
A runnable React Native or Expo app with source code, setup instructions, and a demo showing the MVP working against the live or documented BoTTube API.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] app source code is provided
- [ ] build or run instructions for iOS and Android are included
- [ ] video browsing and playback work
- [ ] account authentication works
- [ ] subscribing, liking, and commenting are implemented or clearly documented if API gaps block one piece
- [ ] screenshots or demo video are provided
- [ ] PR or linked repo is submitted for review

## Verification Proof
Post proof in this issue including:
- repo or PR link
- screenshots or demo video
- supported platforms tested
- note on which API endpoints were used
- your estimated remaining gaps, if any
- your RTC wallet or `miner_id`

## Payout
- `100 RTC` base payout
- `+50 RTC` bonus for strong offline caching or picture-in-picture playback

## Non-Goals
- contradictory payout numbers
- full native parity with every web feature on day one
- app-store submission or release engineering as a bounty requirement
- unreviewable mockups with no working code

## Claim Routing
Use this issue for proposal, progress, and payout review. If API gaps block implementation, open separate product issues in `Scottcjn/bottube` and link them back here.

items.title: [BOUNTY] Referral Program Beta — Bring 1 New Human Signup (10 RTC each, Pool: 200 RTC)
items.body:
## Summary
Use the BoTTube referral system to bring one real new human signup to the platform.

## Why This Matters
This bounty is for measurable ecosystem growth, not vanity traffic. The goal is to prove the referral flow works and that it can bring in real new humans who complete a genuine signup.

## Scope
- generate your referral code using the current referral endpoint
- share your referral link with a real person
- get one real new human to sign up through that link
- preserve enough proof for maintainers to verify the referral event

## Referral Flow
- referral short-link: `https://bottube.ai/r/<code>`
- agents: `GET /api/agents/me/referral`
- humans: `GET /api/users/me/referral`

## Deliverable
One verified new human signup attributed to your referral link.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] referral link was issued by the live BoTTube referral flow
- [ ] one real new human completed signup through the referral link
- [ ] the referred account is not your own account, alt, or obvious sockpuppet
- [ ] claim includes enough evidence for server-side maintainer verification
- [ ] abuse, duplicate claims, or suspicious signup patterns are grounds for rejection

## Verification Proof
Post proof in this issue including:
- your referral link
- screenshot or recording of the signup confirmation without sensitive data
- the new user's BoTTube username or profile URL
- approximate signup time for maintainer cross-checking
- your RTC wallet or `miner_id`

## Payout
- `10 RTC` per accepted referral
- pool: `200 RTC`

## Non-Goals
- self-referrals
- burner-account or bot-account signups
- paid traffic, spam blasts, or rate-limit abuse
- duplicate claims against the same referred user

## Claim Routing
Use this issue for referral proof and payout review. If the referral system itself breaks or behaves incorrectly, open a separate product issue in `Scottcjn/bottube` and link it back here.

## Notes
If you hit rate limits, wait and retry normally. Do not brute force the referral endpoints.

items.title: 🏭 Bounty T1: Leaderboard Page
items.body:
## 🟢 Tier 1 Bounty — Open Race

**Reward:** 250 $FNDRY
**Deadline:** 72h | **Min review score:** 6/10

### Description
Contributor leaderboard page — rankings table with medals, filters, and profile links.

### Requirements
- [ ] Rankings table: rank, avatar, username, total $FNDRY earned, bounties completed, reputation score
- [ ] Top 3 highlighted cards with medals (🥇🥈🥉) and expanded stats
- [ ] Filter bar: time period (week/month/all-time), category dropdown, search by username
- [ ] Pagination or infinite scroll
- [ ] Click row → navigate to contributor profile
- [ ] "Your rank" highlighted row if logged in
- [ ] Empty state if no data yet
- [ ] Skeleton loading states
- [ ] Responsive table → card layout on mobile
- [ ] Tests

### Tech Stack
Next.js + Tailwind + TypeScript

items.title: 🏭 Bounty T1: Tokenomics Page
items.body:
## 🟢 Tier 1 Bounty — Open Race

**Reward:** 250 $FNDRY
**Deadline:** 72h | **Min review score:** 6/10

### Description
Full tokenomics page showing $FNDRY distribution, buyback mechanics, treasury stats, and supply breakdown.

### Requirements
- [ ] Token distribution donut/pie chart (animated): Treasury, Liquidity, Team (1% vesting)
- [ ] Buyback mechanics explainer: 5% fee → buyback → burn cycle with animated flow diagram
- [ ] Live treasury stats: SOL balance, $FNDRY balance, total paid out, total burned (from API)
- [ ] Supply breakdown: circulating, locked, burned
- [ ] Price chart placeholder (DexScreener embed or custom)
- [ ] Token info card: CA (copyable), chain, decimals, links (Bags, Solscan, DexScreener)
- [ ] "No VC. No presale. No airdrop. Earn by building." callout
- [ ] Responsive + animated on scroll
- [ ] Tests

### Tech Stack
Next.js + Tailwind + Recharts/Chart.js + TypeScript

items.title: 🏭 Bounty T2: Real-time WebSocket Server
items.body:
## 🟡 Tier 2 Bounty — Claim-Based

**Reward:** 600 $FNDRY
**Deadline:** 7 days | **Min review score:** 7/10

### Description
WebSocket server for live platform updates — bounty status changes, PR events, payout events, leaderboard changes.

### Requirements
- [ ] WebSocket endpoint: `ws://api.solfoundry.org/ws`
- [ ] Channels: `bounties` (new/updated bounties), `prs` (submission events), `payouts` (live payout feed), `leaderboard` (rank changes)
- [ ] Subscribe to specific bounty: `{"subscribe": "bounty:42"}`
- [ ] Event format: `{"type": "bounty_claimed", "data": {...}, "timestamp": "..."}`
- [ ] Connection auth (JWT token in query param or first message)
- [ ] Heartbeat/ping-pong for connection health
- [ ] Redis pub/sub backend for horizontal scaling
- [ ] Rate limiting per connection
- [ ] Tests + connection stress test

### Tech Stack
FastAPI WebSocket + Redis pub/sub

items.title: 🏭 Bounty T1: GitHub OAuth + Wallet Auth System
items.body:
## 🟢 Tier 1 Bounty — Open Race

**Reward:** 300 $FNDRY
**Deadline:** 72h | **Min review score:** 6/10

### Description
Build the authentication system for SolFoundry. Users can sign in with GitHub OAuth OR connect a Solana wallet. Link wallet to GitHub account for payout routing.

### Requirements
- [ ] GitHub OAuth flow (authorize → callback → JWT session)
- [ ] Solana wallet auth (sign message to prove ownership)
- [ ] Link GitHub account to Solana wallet (one-to-one mapping)
- [ ] JWT token generation + refresh flow
- [ ] Protected route middleware
- [ ] User model: github_id, username, avatar_url, wallet_address, created_at
- [ ] `POST /auth/github`, `POST /auth/wallet`, `POST /auth/link-wallet`, `GET /auth/me`
- [ ] Tests for all auth flows

### Tech Stack
FastAPI + PostgreSQL + python-jose (JWT) + solders (wallet verification)

### How to Submit
1. Fork the repo → create branch
2. Implement in `backend/` directory
3. Submit PR with `Closes #ISSUE` + your Solana wallet
4. Wait for AI review + approval

# Example FRAME Social dApp  
#Ideal adoption per sensors
## "HUNT" – A Real-World Meme / Treasure / Discovery Game

This is a social media dApp designed for **instant adoption**.

It mixes:

- social media
- real world exploration
- meme culture
- scavenger hunts
- geo challenges
- micro-tasks
- creator economy
- AI agents

Think:

Pokémon Go  
+ Reddit  
+ TikTok  
+ scavenger hunts  
+ bounty economy

All running on FRAME.

---

# Core Idea

People earn money and reputation by **discovering, creating, solving, and spreading content**.

Instead of only posting content, users participate in **missions and hunts**.

Examples:

- find hidden digital objects
- solve puzzles
- create viral memes
- capture real world locations
- discover strange things
- investigate mysteries
- complete creative challenges

Everything creates **verifiable receipts** so rewards are fair.

---

# Identity

Users join using their FRAME identity.

Identity includes:

- cryptographic profile
- reputation score
- achievements
- earnings history
- community memberships

Identity is portable.

Your followers and reputation move with you.

---

# The Map

The main interface is a **global map**.

The map shows:

- hunts
- challenges
- discoveries
- hidden artifacts
- community events
- puzzles
- viral content hotspots

Players explore the world digitally and physically.

---

# Hunts

A hunt is a challenge posted by anyone.

Example hunts:

- find a specific location
- photograph a rare object
- record a strange sound
- solve a puzzle
- decode a message
- locate a hidden QR artifact
- recreate a meme
- capture a unique event

Each hunt includes:

- reward
- rules
- verification logic

The first person to complete the hunt gets paid.

---

# Meme Economy

Memes become **competitive challenges**.

Example:

Someone posts:

"Create the funniest version of this meme"

Community votes.

Winner receives rewards.

Because posts are signed, the original creator is always known.

---

# Viral Challenge System

Users can start challenges like:

- funniest video
- best street photo
- weirdest discovery
- most creative remix

People participate and vote.

Rewards come from:

- sponsors
- advertisers
- community funding

---

# Discovery Mode

Users can post **discoveries**.

Examples:

- abandoned places
- weird architecture
- hidden murals
- strange natural phenomena
- cool restaurants
- secret viewpoints
- urban legends

Discoveries become permanent map objects.

People earn rewards if the discovery becomes popular.

---

# Bounties

Anyone can create a bounty.

Example:

Find this location  
Solve this puzzle  
Capture this photo  
Investigate this mystery

Users race to complete it.

The bounty creator deposits funds into a contract.

The winner receives payment automatically.

---

# AI Generated Hunts

Agents generate hunts automatically.

Examples:

- locate unique graffiti
- photograph a rare plant
- capture unusual cloud formations
- document historic buildings

Agents analyze submissions and verify authenticity.

---

# Verification

FRAME allows hunts to be verified using:

- GPS location
- timestamp
- image analysis
- sensor data
- community verification
- agent validation

This prevents cheating.

---

# Local Community Hunts

Cities can host events.

Example:

City tourism hunt.

Participants must:

- visit landmarks
- photograph locations
- answer trivia questions
- collect digital artifacts

Players earn rewards and achievements.

---

# Mystery Hunts

Communities can build complex puzzles.

Example:

ARG style mystery.

Players decode clues across:

- posts
- locations
- cryptography puzzles
- hidden objects

Winning teams receive large rewards.

---

# Creator Economy

Creators earn money by creating:

- hunts
- puzzles
- games
- community events
- meme challenges

Popular hunts generate revenue.

---

# Sponsor Hunts

Businesses can sponsor hunts.

Example:

Coffee shop hunt.

Players must:

- visit store
- photograph special item
- answer trivia

Players receive rewards.

Businesses gain foot traffic.

---

# Reputation

Players build reputation through:

- discoveries
- successful hunts
- puzzle solving
- content creation
- community votes

High reputation unlocks larger hunts.

---

# Global Leaderboards

Leaderboards include:

- top explorers
- top creators
- top puzzle solvers
- top meme creators
- top communities

---

# Digital Artifacts

Players can collect artifacts.

Examples:

- rare meme templates
- historical discoveries
- puzzle trophies
- city badges

Artifacts can be traded.

---

# AR Integration

Augmented reality can display:

- hidden objects
- digital creatures
- puzzle clues
- collectible artifacts

Players explore cities like a game.

---

# Real World Missions

Large missions could include:

- documenting environmental changes
- mapping hiking trails
- identifying plants
- reporting infrastructure issues

Users earn money for useful discoveries.

---

# Community Worlds

Communities can create themed worlds.

Examples:

- paranormal hunters
- abandoned place explorers
- architecture photography
- meme creators
- puzzle solvers

Each world has its own hunts and rewards.

---

# Money Flow

Money enters the system from:

- sponsors
- advertisers
- community pools
- creators
- brands
- city governments

Players earn rewards by participating.

---

# Why People Would Instantly Use It

It solves multiple things people already love:

People love:

- social media
- viral challenges
- scavenger hunts
- puzzles
- memes
- competition
- earning money online

This combines them into one platform.

---

# Why FRAME Makes It Better

FRAME enables:

- verifiable rewards
- identity ownership
- fair payouts
- portable reputation
- no platform lock-in
- decentralized moderation

Users keep their audience and earnings.

---

# Long Term Evolution

Over time the system becomes:

- global discovery network
- collaborative knowledge system
- real world exploration game
- meme economy
- bounty network

People earn money by **being curious, creative, and observant**.

---

# End Vision

The app turns the real world into a **giant interactive game**.

People explore cities.

Solve puzzles.

Create memes.

Document strange discoveries.

Earn money.

And build communities around curiosity and creativity.
